title: Botryopitys meridensis
taxonomy: Botryopitys meridensis belongs to the phylum Tracheophyta, class Pinopsida, and is classified as a fossil fungus within the family of fungi, specifically the order Sebacinales. This fungus is associated with the roots of coniferous trees and is considered to be an ectomycorrhizal fungus. It was named after the location where it was found in Venezuela. The classification of Botryopitys meridensis is based on its morphological features and molecular analysis. The precise classification of this fungus is still evolving with ongoing research and new discoveries in the field of mycology.
